mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over a 20-day forward horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.27% in Dec 2018 reflects recent Fed pivot from tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. The mechanism triggers when unemployment rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, indicating cyclical weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% in Dec 2018, near cyclical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously over a 2-month period, forward equity returns compress as growth expectations fall. This interaction-driven mechanism captures demand-side fragility better than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256k in Nov 2018, revised down to 1181k
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of co-declining instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Sustained month-over-month declines in INDPRO signal supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative equity returns over a 5-day horizon as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.39 in Nov 2018, revised down to 104.07
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth drives consumer income and spending power. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k jobs per month, it signals labor market cooling and reduced household demand. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day equity returns as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150263k in Dec 2018, revised to 149809k
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL interacts with federal funds rate policy. When inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains elevated (> 2.0%), the Fed faces stagflationary pressure and may signal further tightening, compressing equity valuations. This interaction captures policy constraint dynamics.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 252.73 in Dec 2018, FEDFUNDS at 2.27%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic activity. Quarterly GDP growth below 2% annualized signals below-trend expansion and reduced earnings growth, producing negative 60-day equity returns as the market reprices terminal growth rates downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18664.97 in Q2 2018, revised to 20276.15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 68% of sub-2% growth quarters, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction employment. Sustained HOUST above 1.2 million units annualized signals robust housing demand and forward consumer confidence, producing positive 5-day equity returns as the market reprices growth expectations upward in real-time.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256k in Nov 2018, revised to 1181k, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.
